recent_evidence.summary: Weak-pi pODMR terminal result copied for accepted r03; job completed with savedexperiment 1DExp-seq-Rabimodulated-vary-mw_freq-2026-05-13-180435.mat, 4 averages x 50000 repetitions, final counts 43.890 kcps.

Raw/readout-aware weak-pi pODMR review supports a clear usable resonance at 3.876 GHz for r03: raw signal, point-wise ratio, and reference-line normalization all minimize at 3.876 GHz; all 4 averages share the signal minimum; raw signal drop is about 14.3% vs edge median. Average 4 is drift-flagged but does not invalidate the shared resonance-scale dip.

Ramsey/T2star scout model, protocol inspection, and advisory for accepted r03. The 8 us/51 point plan exceeded the 450 s tracking cap, so it was redesigned to 6 us/31 points with det=1.5 MHz, 4 x 50000 shots; revised advisory has no blockers and estimates 417.946 s per average under the 450 s cap.

Started first Ramsey/T2star scout on r03 as nv23_ramsey_20260513_185505_auto_ramsey. It scans tau 0..6 us in 31 points with mw_freq=3.876 GHz, det=1.5 MHz, length_pi_pulse=52 ns, 4 averages x 50000 repetitions. Live status shows running average 1/4 and final-count text 43.890 kcps; bridge occupancy blocks further submissions until terminal.

Weak-pi pODMR accepted; Ramsey/T2star scout started: Weak-pi pODMR on accepted r03 completed and raw/readout-aware review supports a clear usable resonance at 3.876 GHz: raw signal, point-wise ratio, and reference-line normalization all minimize there, all 4 averages share the signal minimum, and raw drop is about 14.3% vs edge median. A first Ramsey/T2star scout is now running as nv23_ramsey_20260513_185505_auto_ramsey using tau 0..6 us in 31 points, det=1.5 MHz, mw_freq=3.876 GHz, 4 x 50000 repetitions. The initial 8 us/51 point Ramsey plan was rejected because advisory exceeded the 450 s tracking cap; the revised preview had no blockers and estimated 417.946 s per average. Next action is to wait for terminal Ramsey result and perform raw/FFT review before any T2star or 13C conclusion.
